questions.explanation: To solve the linear inequality, we isolate x by subtracting 5 from both sides and then dividing by 2. This results in x > 2.

questions.q: If 2x - 3 > 7, then the solution set is?
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides and add 3 to both sides, then divide by 2 to isolate x. This gives 2x > 12, or x > 6. But in this question, we have x > 5.
To solve the linear inequality, we add 2 to both sides, resulting in x < 5. However, in this question, x < 5 and we have x - 2 < 3, which implies x - 2 + 2 < 3 + 2.
Subtract 2 from both sides and then divide by 4 to isolate x. This results in x ≤ 4.
Add 2 to both sides and then divide by 3 to isolate x. This results in x > 3.
Subtract 3 from both sides and then multiply by 2 to isolate x. This results in x ≤ 8.

Distribute the 2 on the left side and then isolate x. This gives 6x - 4 > 20. Add 4 to both sides and then divide by 6 to isolate x. This results in x > 16/3, which is approximately 5.33. But in this question, x > 4.
The absolute value of x is greater than 2. This means that x can be either positive or negative, but the absolute value will always be greater than 2. So, x > 2 or x < -2.

The absolute value of x + 2 is less than or equal to 5. This means that x + 2 can be between -5 and 5, inclusive. So, x + 2 ≥ -5 and x + 2 ≤ 5. Subtract 2 from both sides of each inequality to get x ≥ -7 and x ≤ 3.

The absolute value of 3x - 1 is less than 7. This means that 3x - 1 can be between -7 and 7, exclusive. So, 3x - 1 > -7 and 3x - 1 < 7. Add 1 to both sides of each inequality to get 3x > -6 and 3x < 8. Divide both sides of each inequality by 3 to isolate x. This results in x > -2 and x < 8/3.

Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x - 3)(x - 1) ≥ 0. This means that either both factors are non-negative or both are non-positive. So, (x - 3 ≥ 0 and x - 1 ≥ 0) or (x - 3 ≤ 0 and x - 1 ≤ 0). This gives x ≥ 3 or x ≤ 1.
Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x + 1)^2 < 0. This means that the square is always non-negative, so there is no solution.

Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x - 2)(x - 3) ≥ 0. This means that either both factors are non-negative or both are non-positive. So, (x - 2 ≥ 0 and x - 3 ≥ 0) or (x - 2 ≤ 0 and x - 3 ≤ 0). This gives x ≥ 3 or x ≤ 2.

questions.q: The solution set of the linear inequality 2x + 5 ≥ 11 is?
questions.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides and then divide by 2 to isolate x. This results in x ≥ 3.

Factor the left side of the inequality to get (2x - 3)(x - 1) > 0. This means that either both factors are non-negative or both are non-positive. So, (2x - 3 ≥ 0 and x - 1 ≥ 0) or (2x - 3 ≤ 0 and x - 1 ≤ 0). This gives x > 3/2 or x < 1.
Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x + 2)^2 ≤ 0. This means that the square is always non-negative, so there is no solution.

Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x - 4)(x - 2) ≥ 0. This means that either both factors are non-negative or both are non-positive. So, (x - 4 ≥ 0 and x - 2 ≥ 0) or (x - 4 ≤ 0 and x - 2 ≤ 0). This gives x ≥ 4 or x ≤ 2.

questions.q: The solution set of the linear inequality x^2 + 5x + 6 < 0 is?
questions.explanation: Factor the left side of the inequality to get (x + 3)(x + 2) < 0. This means that one factor is non-negative and the other is non-positive. But since both factors are always non-negative, there is no solution.
